_meta.description: Mapping of finding types to the five Cyber Essentials control themes. Cyber Essentials is the UK government's baseline cyber security certification, required by many public-sector contracts. The scheme has five technical control themes: Firewalls, Secure Configuration, User Access Control, Malware Protection, and Security Update Management.
_meta.mapping_rationale: Cyber Essentials themes are broad. Cloud misconfigurations typically touch Secure Configuration (the default and most-used theme for cloud) and User Access Control (when the misconfiguration affects who can access data). Mappings below reflect the primary theme(s) each finding directly compromises.

audit_notes.S3_PUBLIC_VIA_ACL: Two themes. User Access Control is primary — an AllUsers grant is a direct failure of access control. Secure Configuration is secondary — the bucket was configured insecurely at creation or through subsequent changes.
audit_notes.S3_PUBLIC_ACCESS_BLOCK_DISABLED: Secure Configuration only. PAB is a bucket-level configuration setting; the theme covers exactly this case.
audit_notes.S3_ENCRYPTION_DISABLED: Secure Configuration only. Encryption at rest is a default AWS bucket configuration; a bucket without it has been configured (or left configured) insecurely.
